title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Aeneas Oliver stays with her friend Judith Butler while they help prepare for a Halloween party for children aged ten to seventeen. The party is hosted by Mrs. Royana Drecker, who refers to the event as a "High School Entrance Exam Party" because the children are graduating from Elm Tree School, though Miss Whitaker notes that entrance exams have recently been abolished.

During the preparations, Oliver observes the decorations, including pumpkins and gourds, and reminisces about seeing many pumpkins in America during Thanksgiving. As women arrange furniture and decorations, Oliver discusses the timing of Halloween and All Saints' Day. When a bowl of apples is brought in for an apple-biting game, thirteen-year-old Joyce identifies Oliver as a writer of murder stories. Joyce suggests Oliver create a murder mystery game for the party, but Oliver declines, mentioning a previous unsuccessful attempt.

As the group prepares various games, including tug-of-war and a "dragon game," they decide to use the library because its old carpet can handle moisture. Mrs. Hargreaves brings a green plastic bucket for the games, while Miss Lee suggests a zinc bucket would be more stable. Oliver, who has a dislike for plastic, observes the guests from a chair while eating an apple. She mentally categorizes the attendees, including Miss Johnson (the organist's sister), Royana Drecker, and several children such as Beatrice, Kathy, Diana, and the assertive Joyce. The passage concludes with a discussion about hand mirrors and the possibility of seeing future husbands, as well as Joyce expressing her preference for murder stories with a "strong blood scent."
